title: DoorDash's Q4 Report: Strong Growth, But a Profitability Puzzle
text: DoorDash delivered mixed Q4 2025 results, achieving robust revenue growth of 38% year-over-year to $3.96 billion while missing earnings expectations. The company reported GAAP EPS of $0.48, falling short of the $0.59 consensus estimate. The primary driver of the earnings miss stems from aggressive technology investments totaling several hundred million dollars more in 2026 to unify its international platforms (Wolt and Deliveroo) under a single global tech stack. While this strategic bet promises long-term operational efficiency and reduced delivery costs, it pressures near-term profitability. DoorDash commands 67% of the U.S. food delivery market, supported by 26 million DashPass subscribers. However, competitive pressures from Uber Eats (23-24% market share) and regulatory labor costs pose significant headwinds. Trading at premium multiples with a P/E ratio of 80.22 and EV/EBITDA of 47.94, the stock carries valuations that demand flawless execution.
